articles.summary: 86% of surveyed users say AI helps them better understand their money, but most tools still aren't built around real financial context. With the new personal finance experience in ChatGPT [4], they can now get guidance around spending, saving, and cash flow—all powered by Plaid.

articles.title: with industry-leading safeguards and users stay in control.
articles.summary: This new experience in ChatGPT is part of a shift toward intelligent finance—where AI, secure data connectivity, and consumer trust come together to help people make more informed financial decisions.

articles.title: BEFORE MASS LAYOFFS, META REASSIGNS 7,000 WORKERS TO FOCUS ON AI
articles.summary: Meta is moving 7,000 employees to four new organizations focused on building new AI tools and apps. The organizations will use AI native design structures and have fewer managers per employee than other parts of the company. CEO Mark Zuckerberg has bet the future of Meta on AI. The company plans to spend up to $135 billion in capital expenditures this year, much of it going to building computing facilities that power AI and hiring researchers.

articles.title: JURY REJECTS MUSK'S CLAIMS AGAINST OPENAI
articles.summary: A judge has dismissed Elon Musk's claims against OpenAI. A jury found that Musk brought the lawsuit against the company and Sam Altman after the statute of limitations expired. Musk says he plans to appeal and described the ruling as a destructive precedent. OpenAI now has a clear path to a public listing.

articles.title: UNITREE'S IPO FILING: THE STATE OF THE ROBOTICS MARKET
articles.summary: Unitree Robotics recently filed for IPO on Shanghai's STAR Market. The company is profitable and growing fast. It has shipped more humanoid robots than any other company in the world. This post looks at what Unitree makes, its revenue mix, its customers, its vertically integrated approach, the financial picture, and Unitree's model layer ambitions.

articles.title: HERE'S WHAT THE WI-FI ROUTER FOR AMAZON'S STARLINK RIVAL LOOKS LIKE
articles.summary: The FCC has published images of Amazon's Wi-Fi router for Leo. The router supports Wi-Fi 6 and a mesh mode. The basic-looking, box-shaped router has a power port and two Ethernet ports, one specifically for the Leo dish. It supports Bluetooth Low Energy and the ZigBee wireless protocol.

articles.title: REMEMBER WHEN CLOUD COSTS CAUGHT EVERYONE OFF GUARD? DON'T LET AI DO THE SAME
articles.summary: Datadog's guide to AI ROI [9] breaks down how to proactively monitor AI costs by token, model, provider, and team. Don't wait until the bill comes - put cost data directly in front of the engineers making

articles.title: the decisions that drive spend. Get a complete picture of AI ROI
PROJECT GLASSWING: WHAT MYTHOS SHOWED US
articles.summary: Cloudflare was invited to use Mythos Preview as part of Project Glasswing. The company pointed it at more than 50 of its repositories to see what it would find and to see how it works. This post shares what the company observed, what the model did well and what it didn't, and how it can be improved. Mythos Preview is a real step forward, and its existence raises questions about how security should be handled in the near future.

articles.title: A BROWSER CLI FOR YOUR AI AGENTS
articles.summary: browse is a browser CLI designed to be driven by AI agents. It gives AI agents skills for automating websites. Agents can navigate any page with low-level primitives. Users can see exactly what the page did in real time. All CLI commands work natively with local Chromium. Users have the option to switch to remote sessions.

articles.title: AI EATS THE WORLD
articles.summary: Big tech is pouring around $700 billion into AI capital expenditures this year. Under-investing is seen as the bigger risk. Foundation models are commoditizing fast, and real value is shifting up the stack to applications, agents, and workflows. Adoption is wide, but shallow. Deep integration is still rare outside of tech and finance.

articles.title: META'S GIANT AI DATA CENTER IS RESHAPING RURAL LOUISIANA
articles.summary: Meta's plans to build the world's biggest AI facility have entangled it deeply into Louisiana's politics, culture, and economy. The facility is expected to reach 5 gigawatts of compute capacity. The company plans to spend more than $200 billion on the project. This article looks at the series of deals Meta had to make to even start the project and how the project has impacted the locals in the area.

articles.title: THIS AI MAKES YOUR RAW MEETING NOTES AWESOME. YOU GET 1 MONTH FREE
articles.summary: Granola transcribes your calls without the awkward meeting bot and expands on your thoughts to make your notes awesome. Plus it follows up for you. 1 month free with code: [15]TLDR1MO

articles.summary: The new company aims to bring 500 megawatts of capacity online in 2027 and substantially increase capacity over time.

articles.title: DON'T ANSWER THE FIRST QUESTION
articles.summary: When a user asks something weird, don't answer the first version of the question: figure out what they really meant, then answer that.
